title: Hyperlink Scorecard Entries
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to add hyperlinks to scorecard entries, so that I can navigate directly to related content and tools from within the scorecard.
description:
Scorecard entries need to support hyperlinks, allowing users to link and navigate to related content and tools directly from the scorecard table.

### Requirements
- Add an interface for users to add URLs, potentially within the cell edit popup dialog.
- Users should be able to add a URL without dealing with markup syntax.
- The UX must handle the conflict between editing cell contents and clicking hyperlinked values — the same cell needs to support both interaction modes.

### Design Considerations
- Reference: Sprint 3 Requirements — Scorecard Table Enhancements, Hyperlinked Selections (R&D) section.
acceptance_criteria: User can add a URL to a scorecard entry via the edit popup dialog
URL input accepts plain URLs without requiring markup syntax
Hyperlinked cell values are visually distinguishable from plain text
Clicking a hyperlinked value navigates to the URL without triggering edit mode
Editing a cell with a hyperlink is still possible through the edit dialog
ux_changes: Adds a URL input field to the scorecard cell edit popup. Hyperlinked cell values display as clickable links with distinct visual styling. Click behavior distinguishes between navigation (click) and editing (via popup).
middleware_changes: Server-side logic handles storing and retrieving URL values alongside cell content. Validation ensures well-formed URLs.
implementation_notes: Key UX challenge: the same cell value needs to support both click-to-navigate (hyperlink) and click-to-edit interactions. This requires careful event handling to distinguish user intent.
comment_summary: Ryan requested the label be 'URL' instead of 'Link' for consistency. The developer confirmed and updated the label.
